  "claims": [
    "SB 753 modernizes state law to provide local governments with tools to address abandoned shopping carts more efficiently.",
    "The bill allows cities to return abandoned shopping carts directly to retailers without bureaucratic delays.",
    "Abandoned shopping carts create safety hazards, block sidewalks, pollute waterways, and cost cities time and taxpayer money to clean up.",
    "Current state law has proven ineffective at preventing shopping cart abandonment and incentivizing proper recovery.",
    "Inefficiencies exist in the requirement for cities to store abandoned carts in impound lots and wait for retailers to pick them up."
